title: lotus corniculatus subsp. valdepilosus
intro: Lotus corniculatus subsp. valdepilosus, also known as the lesser birdsfoot trefoil, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, and is believed to have a number of health benefits, including reducing fever, promoting wound healing, and aiding in the treatment of digestive issues. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal and health benefits, and it should be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
